canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, Yelp is consistently described as a dataset composed of user reviews from the Yelp platform, often characterized as containing opinions on restaurants, services, and businesses. The prevailing application of this dataset is for sentiment classification and analysis tasks, where it is used to evaluate downstream classifiers. For example, one study frames its use as a 5-way text classification task to assess the utility of synthetic data. While sentiment analysis is the most common focus, a smaller set of studies employs the dataset for different purposes. One paper uses Yelp for out-of-distribution evaluation of sentiment analysis models. Another study utilizes its collection of "casual reviews" to evaluate text origin detection methods on short-form natural language.
papers: Scalable Vision Language Model Training via High Quality Data Curation
Unraveling the Mechanics of Learning-Based Demonstration Selection for In-Context Learning
Pluralistic Alignment for Healthcare: A Role-Driven Framework
Social Genome: Grounded Social Reasoning Abilities of Multimodal Models
SCISSOR: Mitigating Semantic Bias through Cluster-Aware Siamese Networks for Robust Classification
Synthesizing Privacy-Preserving Text Data via Finetuning *without* Finetuning Billion-Scale LLMs
A Template Is All You Meme
CSR-Bench: BenchmarkingLLMAgents in Deployment of Computer Science Research Repositories

definitions.text: A dataset derived from Yelp reviews, used for sentiment classification tasks to source seed samples and evaluate downstream classifiers.
definitions.paper: A Template Is All You Meme
